column1: # One shared table of (env, input, excluded) -> expected output, consumed
# twice so the two §5 resolve() implementations stay honest against each
#     drives the C++ REPL: env lines are piped in (forward AND reversed
#     definition order — the order-independence property), then the probe:
#     the bare input when nothing is excluded, or `subs input, v=v, ...`
#     (explicit no-op substitutions are exactly the §7 exclusion mechanism).
#     Rows with a csv also pin the "resolution IS subs" property: a fresh
#     session running `subs input, <csv>` must print the same output.
#     wasm build: closure() + one `subs` binding call must yield `expected`,
#     the active set must serialize to the same assignment set as `csv` in a
#     valid parents-first order, and the same env in reversed order must
#   env       ';'-separated definitions in `name := value` form (values are
#             canonical §5 plain prints; expressions only — equation values
#   input     the expression whose resolution is probed
#   excluded  ','-separated designated symbols, or `-` for none
#   csv       the parents-first `subs` serialization of the active set for
#             the forward env order, or `-` when nothing applies
#   expected  the simplified resolved plain output both frontends must print
